headline: Mastercard Scrutiny And Asset Sale Reframe Growth And Valuation Story
summary: Mastercard (NYSE:MA) has received formal warning letters from the US Federal Trade Commission over alleged debanking practices tied to executive orders. The company is preparing to sell its real time payments unit acquired from Nets Group, reversing course on its largest acquisition. Both developments raise questions about Mastercard's regulatory risk profile and future focus within the payments ecosystem. For investors tracking Mastercard at a current share price of $500.75, these moves...

headline: FTC warns PayPal, Visa, Mastercard and Stripe against debanking after Trump’s executive order
summary: The Federal Trade Commission has warned PayPal, Visa, Mastercard, and Stripe against debanking practices. President Trump and his sons have frequently bemoaned debanking practices. The Trump family claims it got into crypto because of unfair treatment from banks.

headline: Dollar still king as euro stablecoin volumes halve. Here‘s why, say analysts
summary: Euro-denominated stablecoin spot volumes have plunged, according to a new report. The tokens haven’t gained traction in the eurozone, where traders prefer dollar-dominated cryptocurrencies. Still, some think the euro-backed stablecoin market is poised for rapid growth.
